extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter won Stage 2 in La Roche-sur-Yon after GAVIRIA Fernando crashed under 2km, positioning sixth wheel before launching uphill sprint
tactical_patterns: SAGAN positioned sixth wheel before uphill finish rather than typical second/third, allowing acceleration runway on gradual gradient sprint
Quick-Step ran leadout without their sprinter GAVIRIA after crash, ALAPHILIPPE still committing resources despite no finisher present
COLBRELLI Sonny delayed jump on SAGAN's wheel costing victory; uphill drag punishes hesitation more than flat sprint positioning errors
DÉMARE Arnaud rejoined lead sprint group after puncture at 20km, indicating echelon/selection never hard enough to eliminate mechanicals

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: won exhausted on 35°C day after 30km team pacemaking, suggesting exceptional heat tolerance versus pure sprinters
rider_signals.GAVIRIA Fernando: negative: crashed under 2km in yellow jersey, second consecutive stage affected by positioning lapses in technical finals
rider_signals.COLBRELLI Sonny: positive: matched SAGAN on uphill sprint but tactical hesitation cost win; pure speed competitive with world champion
rider_signals.DÉMARE Arnaud: positive: survived 20km chase from puncture to contest sprint, exceptional resilience but indicates weak team support
rider_signals.GREIPEL André: negative: present in final group but failed to contest sprint meaningfully, power insufficient for uphill drag finish
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: neutral: continued working in final despite GAVIRIA crash eliminating Quick-Step sprint chance, team commitment over individual preservation
